title: Today's Tech Brief: Cisco sings Mythos' praises - but doesn't say how many bugs the model uncovered
summary: Cisco praises AI bug hunting model, Microsoft extends olive branch to 0-day researcher, and Anthropic adds partners to Project Glasswing.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Cisco sings Mythos' praises - but doesn't say how many bugs the model uncovered
imageCaption: Cisco sings Mythos' praises - but doesn't say how many bugs the model uncovered
imageSourceTitle: Cisco sings Mythos' praises - but doesn't say how many bugs the model uncovered

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the tech headlines. First up, Cisco is singing the praises of an AI model from Mythos that's designed to hunt down bugs in code. The model is apparently quite good at its job, but Cisco isn't saying just how many bugs it uncovered. I'm curious - if it's that effective, you'd think they'd want to shout it from the rooftops. It's a bit like when your friend tells you about a great new restaurant, but won't give you any details about the food. You're left wondering, 'Is it really that good?'

Next, Microsoft is extending an olive branch to a 0-day researcher who recently got into a public dustup with the company. It seems Redmond has dialed back its rhetoric and is insisting that vulnerability hunters aren't in its legal crosshairs. A calm and rational approach is usually the best way to handle these things - let's hope it works out. I've seen my fair share of online spats, and it's always better when both parties take a step back and try to find common ground.

Cisco sings Mythos' praises - but doesn't say how many bugs the model uncovered is also worth a quick mention. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
